question.label: Direction
question.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?
question.description: Probes whether they have a real picture of where they want to go.

answer: I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.

assessment.note: He names a specific self-limiting pattern — not being explicit about desired opportunities — which signals stalled growth and mildly reduced agency; also implies a clarity issue off-signature.

new_queue.label: Opportunity ask
new_queue.name: What opportunities have you wanted but not named out loud?

new_queue.label: Trust gap
new_queue.name: Where does stakeholder trust feel thinnest for you right now?

new_queue.label: Executor mode
new_queue.name: Where are you still operating more like an executor than a strategic owner?

new_queue.label: Next move
new_queue.name: What would your next move look like before we next meet?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 5)

history.answer_excerpt: When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
